subtasks.description: Query Salesforce data warehouse to retrieve licensed seat count and actual seat assignments (assigned_users) by seat type/license level, including counts of active and inactive assignments

Analyze licensed vs assigned seats to calculate utilization rate, identify unused/unutilized licenses, and generate utilization posture (over-licensed, under-licensed, optimized) with seat count gaps and recommendations

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks: (1) sql_gen at mid tier to query Salesforce seat data with grouping and aggregations across license types; (2) cross_recon at mid tier to reconcile licensed vs actual seat assignments and generate utilization analysis with classification. No policy_action needed—utilization analysis is not a governance decision. No extract needed—data comes from Salesforce warehouse, not documents. Minimum count justified: cannot fully analyze without retrieving both dimensions of data first, then reconciling. Cost-efficient: single SQL query with multi-table joins (licensed seats table + assignment table) returns all needed columns; cross_recon performs comparative analysis and posture classification in one pass.

output.sql: SELECT product AS license_level, license_id, SUM(daily_active_seats) AS licensed_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS assigned_users, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN event_type = 'active' THEN user_id END) AS active_assignments, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN event_type = 'inactive' THEN user_id END) AS inactive_assignments, SUM(daily_active_seats) - COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unutilized_licenses FROM consumption_facts WHERE publisher = 'Salesforce' AND event_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY product, license_id ORDER BY unutilized_licenses DESC
